candidates.title: Best Paper Award
candidates.description: For the paper "Sorting Out the Document Identifier Assignment Problem" at the 29th ECIR 2007, Rome.

candidates.title: Yahoo! Faculty Research and Engagement Program Award
candidates.description: Recipient of the Yahoo! FREP 2011 in the area of Computational Advertising, Machine Learning and Search & Web Mining, while at ISTI-CNR.

candidates.title: Test of Time Award
candidates.description: Awarded at ECIR 2018 (Grenoble) for an article originally published in 2007 (the ECIR 2007 best paper on document identifier assignment).

candidates.title: Best Paper Award (HCAI Workshop)
candidates.description: For "Enhancing XAI Narratives through Multi-Narrative Refinement and Knowledge Distillation" (Giorgi, M. Silvestri, Tolomei, F. Silvestri, Campagnano).

notes: Silvestri's DIAG bio (https://diag.uniroma1.it/users/fabrizio_silvestri) claims he holds 'three best paper awards and other international awards' in addition to the ECIR 2018 Test-of-Time. I could verify only two best-paper awards from institutional sources: ECIR 2007 (main conference, confirmed by CNR) and the HCAI @ CIKM 2025 workshop (confirmed by Sapienza DI and HERCOLE Lab). A third best-paper award is referenced by his bio but I could not pin it to a specific venue/year via a credible source in this session — a human reviewer should follow up (candidates to investigate: earlier ISTI-CNR-era workshop papers with Perego/Orlando/Lucchese, or Yahoo/Facebook-era papers). The DIAG bio also mentions nine industrial patents, which are not awards. Namesakes filtered out: a runner (FIDAL Ascoli Marathon), a tennis player on ITF, and any radio/journalism profiles — none of these match the CS professor. web_search quota was exhausted before I could exhaust all queries; recommend re-running searches for '"Fabrizio Silvestri" best paper' scoped to CIKM/WSDM/SIGIR/ECIR proceedings and ISTI-CNR news archives to locate the third best-paper award.
